theme: The Office Knows Things — Surface Context, Reward Momentum, Bury Secrets

ideas.title: Desk Hover Rich Tooltip — The Office Speaks When You Hover
ideas.description: Currently the office view is visually rich but informationally opaque: you can see who is at a desk but not what they are doing without switching tabs. Fix this with a rich floating mini-card on desk Character hover. In Office.jsx, in the Character component add state `const [miniCardVisible, setMiniCardVisible] = useState(false)`. For non-manager, non-ghost variants, set visible true onMouseEnter and false onMouseLeave. Render a `.desk-mincard` div inside the char when visible: position:absolute; bottom:calc(100% + 10px); left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); width:190px; z-index:200; pointer-events:none; background:var(--surface); border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:10px; padding:10px 12px; box-shadow:0 6px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.45); font-size:0.77rem; animation:mincard-in 0.15s ease. Content: worker name (font-weight:700, 0.85rem), role (color:var(--text-muted), 0.72rem, margin-bottom:6px), task truncated to 72 chars (color:var(--text), line-height:1.3, margin-bottom:8px), then a 4px mini progress bar (border-radius:99px; background:var(--surface2); fill div width:min(progress,100)% with status-matching gradient). Add CSS downward arrow via ::after: position:absolute; bottom:-6px; left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); border:6px solid transparent; border-top-color:var(--border); content:''. Pass progress and status as props to Character. Add to Office.css: @keyframes mincard-in { from { opacity:0; transform:translateX(-50%) translateY(4px); } to { opacity:1; transform:translateX(-50%) translateY(0); } }. WHY: The office floor is the product hero — beautiful theater with zero subtitles. A rich desk tooltip makes the office a real monitoring instrument. Users stop tab-switching and stay on Office. It is the upgrade that makes the visual layer earn its existence.

ideas.title: Vibe Trajectory Sparkline — Was Crushing It All Day?
ideas.description: The Team Vibe stat card shows the current label, but Crushing It tells you nothing about trajectory — did the team just recover from On Fire, or have they been crushing for 10 syncs? A sparkline answers this. In App.jsx add const vibeHistoryRef = useRef([]). On each successful sync, after computing vibe, append { key: vibe.key, t: Date.now() } capping at 20 entries. Pass vibeHistory={vibeHistoryRef.current} to StatsBar. Inside the .stat-card.vibe after the vibe-value span, render a 68x18px SVG with aria-label Vibe trend. Map vibe keys to Y (lower Y = better): crushing=2, in-flow=6, on-fire=10, slow-day=13, after-hours=16. Compute xStep = 67 / Math.max(history.length-1, 1). Build a points string for a polyline element. Also render a filled path from the polyline shape down to y=18 back to origin with fill=var(--accent) opacity=0.12. Render the polyline: stroke=var(--accent), strokeWidth=1.5, fill=none, strokeLinecap=round, strokeLinejoin=round. Add a circle at the last point: r=2.5, fill=var(--accent). Add a title child element with text Vibe over last N syncs. CSS: .vibe-sparkline { display:block; margin-top:5px; overflow:visible; } .stat-card.vibe:hover .vibe-sparkline circle { r:4; transition:r 0.2s; }. Only render when history.length >= 2. WHY: A vibe label is a snapshot. A sparkline is a story. Trend context transforms the stats bar from a scoreboard into a narrative. It is the smallest possible addition that makes the most analytical users feel seen.

ideas.title: Progress Velocity Chip — Grinding, Stalled, or Ghost?
ideas.description: Carlos at 10% after 3 hours and Maya at 0% after 34 minutes are completely invisible today — their progress bars look identical to someone who just hit those numbers. Add per-worker velocity tracking. In App.jsx add const prevProgressRef = useRef({}) (map of worker.id to progress). On each sync after allWorkers updates: for each worker where status==='working', compute delta = worker.progress - (prevProgressRef.current[worker.id] ?? worker.progress). Store in progressDeltaMap state keyed by id. After computing all deltas update prevProgressRef. Pass progressDelta={progressDeltaMap[worker.id] ?? null} into WorkerCard then ProgressBar. In ProgressBar, when progressDelta is not null and status==='working', render a .progress-velocity chip in the progress-header row (margin-left:auto): delta > 0 => class pv--up text triangle +N%; delta === 0 => class pv--stalled text circle Stalled; delta < 0 => class pv--down text inverted-triangle -N%. CSS: .progress-velocity { font-size:0.7rem; font-weight:700; padding:2px 7px; border-radius:99px; letter-spacing:0.02em; margin-left:0.5rem; } .pv--up { background:rgba(34,197,94,0.15); color:var(--green); animation:pv-pop 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1); } .pv--stalled { background:rgba(245,158,11,0.12); color:var(--yellow); } .pv--down { background:rgba(239,68,68,0.12); color:var(--red); } @keyframes pv-pop { from { transform:scale(0.7); opacity:0; } to { transform:scale(1); opacity:1; } }. Skip rendering on first sync when no previous data exists. WHY: Progress bars without velocity are photographs. With velocity they are speedometers. The stalled chip is the most valuable output — the closest this dashboard gets to automatically surfacing who might need help.

ideas.title: Manager Monitor Pixel Vibe Display — Bloberto's Ticker
ideas.description: The .mgr-desk__monitor has data-vibe={vibe} applied but renders as a flat colored rectangle — prime office real estate that every eye crosses and ignores. Make it a tiny animated data display. In Office.jsx, convert the self-closing div to a parent and add a .mgr-monitor__screen child containing a 28x14px SVG. Per vibe: crushing => 3 rect bar charts (x:4/12/20, fill:#4ade80) each with @keyframes bar-dance { 0%,100% { transform:scaleY(0.85); } 50% { transform:scaleY(1.1); } } staggered 0.3s, transform-origin:bottom center; on-fire => a circle cx=14 cy=7 r=4 fill=#ef4444 with @keyframes dot-urgent { 0%,100% { r:3; opacity:1; } 50% { r:6; opacity:0.45; } } plus double exclamation glyph at 5px white; in-flow => a 9-point sine-wave polyline spanning x:2-26 with stroke:#818cf8 and stroke-dasharray:40, animated with @keyframes wave-march { to { stroke-dashoffset:-40; } } 1.5s linear infinite; slow-day => a horizontal line at y=8 stroke=#f59e0b plus a spike line at x=14 with @keyframes flatline-spike { 0%,85%,100% { opacity:0; } 87%,97% { opacity:1; } }; after-hours => crescent moon path fill=#fde68a plus 3 tiny star circles fading alternately. CSS: .mgr-monitor__screen { width:100%; height:100%; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; overflow:hidden; border-radius:2px; }. WHY: The manager monitor is the visual center of gravity of the office floor and it is a blank box. A vibe-specific animated display at pixel-scale makes Bloberto feel like he has a live feed. Users lean in to read it. It rewards attention with discovery.

ideas.title: Tab Switch Wave Card Entrance — The Team Checks In
ideas.description: Switching from Office to Dashboard makes all worker cards exist instantly. There is zero ceremony. Add a staggered spring wave. In App.jsx add const [dashTabKey, setDashTabKey] = useState(0). In the tab-switching handler, when switching TO dashboard, call setDashTabKey(k => k + 1). Pass dashTabKey as a prop to each WorkerCard. In WorkerCard, add a useEffect that fires when dashTabKey changes: add className worker-card--wave-in to the card div via a ref, then remove it after 700ms via setTimeout. The card already has style={{ '--i': index }} — use it for stagger. In App.css: .worker-card--wave-in { animation: card-wave-in 0.45s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) both; animation-delay: calc(var(--i, 0) * 0.07s); will-change: transform, opacity; } @keyframes card-wave-in { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(22px) scale(0.93); } to { opacity:1; transform:none; } }. Also animate stat-bar cards on switch: .stat-card--wave-in { animation: card-wave-in 0.4s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) both; animation-delay: calc(var(--stat-i, 0) * 0.05s); } with --stat-i 0 through 4 set inline. Remove the wave class after the animation completes to avoid re-trigger on other re-renders. WHY: Dashboards are opened and closed repeatedly during standups. The wave entrance creates a theatrical moment every single time — cards arrive in a ripple, like a team filing into a meeting room. The 1.56 spring overshoot gives it physical weight. Total animation budget is 500ms. Perceived performance cost is zero.

ideas.title: Idle Worker Auto-Generated Away Messages — Where Did They Go?
ideas.description: Idle workers show their frozen last task as if still mid-sentence. Sofia card reads QA review done with status IDLE — she clearly finished and left, but the card implies she is still there. Replace idle task display with time-aware away messages. Add to WorkerCard or App.jsx: function generateAwayMessage(worker) { if (!worker.updated_at) return null; const mins = Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(worker.updated_at)) / 60000); if (mins < 3) return { icon: chat-bubble, text: 'Stepped away from: ' + worker.task.slice(0,42) + '...' }; if (mins < 15) return { icon: coffee, text: 'Probably grabbed a coffee' }; if (mins < 30) return { icon: calendar, text: 'Likely pulled into a meeting' }; if (mins < 60) return { icon: headphones, text: 'Deep research mode' }; if (mins < 120) return { icon: leaf, text: 'Taking a breather' }; return { icon: fishing, text: 'Gone fishing' }; }. In WorkerCard front face, when worker.status === 'idle', replace the .worker-task inner content with the away message styled as italic muted text, plus a collapsed details/summary element showing the actual last task for reference. CSS: .worker-away-msg { font-style:italic; color:var(--text-muted); font-size:0.83rem; display:flex; gap:0.5rem; align-items:flex-start; } .worker-task-details summary { font-size:0.72rem; color:var(--text-muted); cursor:pointer; margin-top:0.5rem; opacity:0.6; } .worker-task-details summary:hover { opacity:1; }. WHY: Gone fishing beats a stale task description every time. It is honest, it is funny, it matches how real offices work. Sofia at 15m idle becomes alive instead of confusingly frozen. One small function, maximum personality per line of code.

ideas.title: Worker Sort + Filter Toolbar — Find the Fire Instantly
ideas.description: With 6+ workers, locating the one in error requires scanning the full grid. Add a compact sort and filter toolbar above the workers-grid. In App.jsx add const [workerSort, setWorkerSort] = useState('status') and const [workerFilter, setWorkerFilter] = useState('all'). Derive displayedRoster via useMemo: filter first (when workerFilter is not 'all', keep only workers where status===workerFilter), then sort: 'status' uses STATUS_ORDER = ['working','idle','done','error']; 'progress' sorts by worker.progress descending; 'duration' sorts by new Date(worker.updated_at) ascending (longest-running first); 'name' sorts alphabetically. Render a WorkerControls component above the grid: a 'Filter:' label, chip buttons [All / Working / Idle / Error / Done] each toggling workerFilter, and a sort select element with options [Status / Progress high-low / Duration / Name]. CSS: .worker-controls { display:flex; gap:0.5rem; align-items:center; flex-wrap:wrap; margin-bottom:1rem; } .filter-chip { padding:3px 10px; border-radius:99px; font-size:0.75rem; font-weight:600; border:1px solid var(--border); background:transparent; color:var(--text-muted); cursor:pointer; transition:all 0.15s; } .filter-chip:hover { border-color:var(--accent); color:var(--text); } .filter-chip--active { background:var(--accent); border-color:var(--accent); color:#fff; } .sort-select { font-size:0.75rem; background:var(--surface); border:1px solid var(--border); color:var(--text); border-radius:6px; padding:3px 8px; cursor:pointer; }. Update the Active Workers heading to show filtered count: Active Workers (3 of 6). Persist sort and filter in localStorage. WHY: During incidents, click Error to see only the broken workers. During standups, sort by Duration to know who has been grinding longest. This is the feature the manager opens when something is wrong and needs answers in under two seconds.

ideas.title: Coffee Corner Break Ticker — The Office Has a Metabolism
ideas.description: The coffee corner is the best prop on the office floor and it does absolutely nothing. In Office.jsx compute: const coffeeTally = workers.filter(w => w.status==='working' && w.updated_at).reduce((sum, w) => sum + Math.max(0, Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(w.updated_at)) / (30 * 60000))), 0). Track prevTallyRef = useRef(0) and coffeeAnimating state. In a useEffect watching coffeeTally: when coffeeTally > prevTallyRef.current, set coffeeAnimating true, setTimeout to false after 1200ms, update prevTallyRef. In the coffee-corner JSX add a badge span.coffee-count when coffeeTally > 0, and apply .coffee-steam-anim to the emoji span when coffeeAnimating. On the coffee-corner div, set data-coffee-label attribute to a string like 'N coffee breaks today' and reveal it via CSS ::after on hover. CSS: .coffee-corner { position:relative; } .coffee-count { position:absolute; top:-5px; right:-5px; background:var(--accent); color:#fff; border-radius:99px; font-size:0.62rem; font-weight:800; padding:1px 5px; min-width:16px; text-align:center; line-height:1.4; pointer-events:none; } .coffee-corner__emoji { display:inline-block; } .coffee-steam-anim .coffee-corner__emoji { animation:coffee-steam 1.2s ease; } @keyframes coffee-steam { 0%,100% { transform:translateY(0) rotate(0deg); } 25% { transform:translateY(-4px) rotate(-4deg); } 75% { transform:translateY(-2px) rotate(4deg); } } .coffee-corner:hover::after { content:attr(data-coffee-label); position:absolute; bottom:calc(100% + 6px); left:50%; transform:translateX(-50%); background:var(--surface); border:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:6px; padding:4px 8px; font-size:0.72rem; white-space:nowrap; pointer-events:none; }. WHY: Props that do nothing erode the world. A tally badge that increments as the team works makes the coffee corner a passive clock — warm and humanizing. These are people who need coffee, not just progress bars.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Auto-Standup Draft Generator — The Boss Writes It For You
ideas.description: Add a small clipboard button (aria-label: Generate standup draft) in header-right, before the theme toggle. On click, generate a formatted standup summary entirely from the current workers state with zero API calls. The generator in App.jsx takes workers, vibe, and vibeStreak and produces a multi-line string: a date header, a divider, the current team vibe with streak count, then sections for In Progress (working workers with name, role, task, progress percent, and duration), Done, Needs Attention (error workers), and Away (idle workers with idle duration). Close with an em-dash signature for Bloberto and a random vibe-appropriate Bloberto quote. Filter nulls and join with newlines. Render in a .standup-drawer (position:fixed; bottom:0; left:0; right:0; max-height:55vh; background:var(--surface); border-top:1px solid var(--border); border-radius:12px 12px 0 0; padding:1.5rem 1.5rem 2rem; z-index:500; overflow-y:auto; transform:translateY(100%) closed and translateY(0) open; transition:transform 0.35s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1)). Inside: a drag-handle div, a pre element (font-family:Courier New; font-size:0.8rem; white-space:pre-wrap; color:var(--text)), a Copy button that calls navigator.clipboard.writeText and swaps its label to Copied for 2s, and a close button. Close on Escape keydown. Generate fresh content on each open. WHY: Engineers hate writing standups. The first time someone opens this at 9:59am with the team waiting and it is actually accurate, they will never close this tab again. Bloberto stops being a mascot and starts earning his keep.

ideas.title: DJ Mode — Secret Vibe Override Easter Egg
ideas.description: Bury a secret for developers to discover. Typing D then J (within 500ms, not inside any input, textarea, or select) anywhere on the page opens the DJ panel. In App.jsx existing keydown handler, add keyBufferRef = useRef('') and a keyBufferTimerRef. On each qualifying keydown: append e.key.toLowerCase() to the buffer, clear and reset a 500ms timeout to blank it. If the buffer ends with 'dj', set isDJMode state true and clear buffer. Render a .dj-panel div (position:fixed; bottom:84px; right:20px; width:176px; background:var(--surface); border:1px solid var(--accent); border-radius:14px; padding:14px; z-index:600; box-shadow:0 8px 32px rgba(0,0,0,0.5); animation:dj-drop 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)) when isDJMode is true. Content: heading DJ Mode (font-weight:700; 0.9rem), subtext Vibe override times 5 min (muted 0.72rem margin-bottom:10px), 5 vibe buttons in a column (each shows vibe emoji and label, border:1px solid transparent, active vibe gets border-color:var(--accent)), a countdown bar (height:3px; background:var(--border); inner div background:var(--accent); transition:width 1s linear; driven from 100% to 0% over 300 ticks of a setInterval), and muted ESC to exit label. Store djVibeOverride state. Derive activeVibe = djVibeOverride ? allVibes.find(v => v.key===djVibeOverride) : vibe and thread it through to all vibe consumers. When countdown hits 0: clear override, close panel, show 2s toast Back on air. Close also on Escape. Track discovery count in localStorage key bloberto-dj-count. @keyframes dj-drop { from { opacity:0; transform:translateY(16px) scale(0.92); } to { opacity:1; transform:none; } }. WHY: Easter eggs are the love language of developers. DJ mode rewards the curious — only someone instinctively typing on a vibe-forward dashboard finds it. The first person who discovers it will immediately ping a teammate. It makes the codebase feel like it has a soul.
